description_long: This is a vintage military-style propeller airplane with a streamlined fuselage painted in a two-tone blue and gray color scheme. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, featuring white insignia and markings typical of World War II-era U.S. Navy aircraft. The cockpit is enclosed with a clear canopy and is positioned just ahead of the wing root, with visible framing and a single pilot seat. The tail section includes a vertical stabilizer with red and white stripes and a conventional horizontal stabilizer. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels and a tail wheel, all exposed. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the engine cowling is rounded, suggesting a piston engine configuration.
description: A vintage military propeller airplane with a blue-gray body, mid-mounted straight wings, and a striped tail, featuring an enclosed cockpit and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile, including the propeller, cockpit canopy, wing insignia, and striped vertical stabilizer.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the propeller, symmetrical wing span, and cockpit canopy centered above the fuselage.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other profile, with visible landing gear, cockpit, and tail markings.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail structure, horizontal stabilizer, and the back of the cockpit canopy.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight, unswept wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a framed canopy and clear side windows.
